zh.lifeSummary: 當Prudence Child於1729年7月22日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可時，她的父親Joseph Child33歲，母親Abigail Bridges32歲。她於1752年7月15日與Uriah Allard結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1803年4月16日在美國康乃狄克州文達哈姆武德斯托可享年73歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州文達哈姆武德斯托可易亞斯特 武德斯托可 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Prudence Child于1729年7月22日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可时，她的父亲Joseph Child33岁，母亲Abigail Bridges32岁。她于1752年7月15日与Uriah Allard结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1803年4月16日在美国康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可享年73岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州文達哈姆武德斯托可易亞斯特 武德斯托可 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Child
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Chill
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Child   or   Chell  .

variants.name: Childs
meanings: English: variant of Child , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Chilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Chilton, for example in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, County Durham, Hampshire, Kent, Shropshire, Somerset, Suffolk, and Wiltshire. The majority are shown by early forms to derive from Old English cild ‘child’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. One place of this name in Somerset possibly gets its first element from Old English cealc ‘chalk, limestone’, and one on the Isle of Wight from the personal name Cēola (compare Chilcott ), or from Old English ceole ‘deep valley’.

variants.name: Bambino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Angelo.

Italian: from Bambino, a byname meaning ‘child’ (see Child ), or a personal name given with reference to Gesù Bambino ‘the Christ child’, the use of Gesù as a personal name being considered taboo in Italy.

variants.name: Chilson
meanings: English: habitational name from Chilson in Oxfordshire, named with Old English cild ‘young man’ (see Child ) + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

History: It is not known when this surname was first brought to America, but it was already established in CT in the early 18th century. Daniel Chilson of Weathersfield, CT, was born c. 1720 and on 4 October 1745 married Sybil Stanclift in Middlesex County, CT.

variants.name: Fairchild
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English fair child ‘handsome child’ or ‘handsome youth of noble birth’ (see Child ).

variants.name: Childers
meanings: English: probably a habitational name from some lost place called Childerhouse, from Old English cildra, genitive plural of cild ‘child’ + hūs ‘house’. This may have referred to some form of orphanage perhaps run by a religious order, or perhaps the first element is to be understood in its later sense as a term of status (see Child ).

nameMeanings.name: Prudence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a medieval form of the Latin name Prudentia, a feminine form of Prudentius, from prudens ‘provident’. The Blessed Prudentia was a 15th-century abbess who founded a new convent at Como in Italy. Later, among the Puritans in 17th-century England, Prudence was associated with the vocabulary word for the quality.

historicEvents.description: Oldest grave seen in the Memorials list.
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: British Forces Capture Fort Griswold
historicEvents.description: The capture of Fort Griswold was the final act of treason that Benedict Arnold committed. This would be a British victory. On the American side 85 were killed, 35 wounded and paroled, 28 taken prisoner, 13 escaped, and 1 twelve year old was captured and released.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of the Battle of Groton and The Burning of New London

historicEvents.title: The Making of the U.S. Constitution.
historicEvents.description: The Philadelphia Convention was intended to be the first meeting to establish the first system of government under the Articles of Confederation. From this Convention, the Constitution of the United States was made and then put into place making it one of the major events in all American History.

historicEvents.title: Connecticut Becomes the 5th State
historicEvents.description: Connecticut became a state on January 9, 1788. In 1650, before it was a state, the boundary of Connecticut ran north from the westside of Greenwich Bay and the coast of the Pacific Ocean. During the 1600s, Westmoreland County was in Connecticut when the boundaries were changed Westmoreland County went to Pennsylvania.

en.lifeSummary: When Prudence Child was born on 22 July 1729, in Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, her father, Joseph Child, was 33 and her mother, Abigail Bridges, was 32. She married Uriah Allard on 15 July 1752. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 16 April 1803, in Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 73, and was buried in East Woodstock Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Prudence Child est née le 22 juillet 1729, à Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Joseph Child, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Abigail Bridges, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Uriah Allard le 15 juillet 1752. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 avril 1803, à Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, États-Unis, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à East Woodstock Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, États-Unis.

Lei sposato Uriah Allard il 15 luglio 1752. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Prudence Childが1729年7月22日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ウィンドハムウッドストックで生まれた時，父Joseph Childは33歳，母Abigail Bridgesは32歳であった。1752年7月15日にUriah Allardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1803年4月16日にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ウィンドハムウッドストックで73歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ウィンドハムウッドストックエアスト ウッドストック セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Prudence Child(이)가 1729년 7월 22일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 윈드함 우드스톡에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Joseph Child(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Abigail Bridges(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1752년 7월 15일 Uriah Allard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1803년 4월 16일 미국 코네티컷 주 윈드함 우드스톡에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 윈드함 우드스톡 이스트 우드스톡 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Prudence Child nasceu em 22 de julho de 1729, em Woodstock, Windham, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, Joseph Child, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Abigail Bridges, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Uriah Allard em 15 de julho de 1752. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de abril de 1803, em Woodstock, Windham, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em East Woodstock Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Prudence Child родилась 22 июля 1729 г. в местности вудстокк, виндхам, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Joseph Child было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Abigail Bridges, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Uriah Allard 15 июля 1752 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 16 апреля 1803 г. в местности вудстокк, виндхам, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности эаст вудстокк кеметири, вудстокк, виндхам, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Prudence Child nació el 22 de julio de 1729, en Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Joseph Child, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Abigail Bridges, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Uriah Allard el 15 de julio de 1752. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 16 de abril de 1803, en Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en East Woodstock Cemetery, Woodstock, Windham, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
